title: Richly Comforting Argentinian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Argentinian dish featuring lamb shoulder, this stew is rich in herbal flavors and tender meat. It is a perfect meal for a cold evening.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the dried oregano, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the beef broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the Dutch oven, and simmer for 1.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Add the uncooked rice to the pot and continue to simmer, covered, for an additional 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
